q: A book at rest on a table is an example of _______ equilibrium.
Inside the sealed soda bottle, the two forms of carbon dioxide are in equilibrium.
Equilibrium is said to be dynamic because the _______.
Which of a following reaction is reversible?
a: fuels are burned in the air
decomposition of calcium carbonate in open vessel
synthesis of ammonia by Haber process

q: If both the forward and reverse reaction rates are equal in the equilibrium is said to be _______.
The reaction where reactants are converted into products is _______ reaction.
The reaction proceeding to the left is called the _______ reaction.
The reactions in which products can turn back into reactants are _______ reactions.
The equilibrium in a chemical reaction is represented by the symbol _______.
At dynamic equilibrium the concentration of both the reactants and products are _______.
In which of the following conditions do you think the rates of both forward and backward reactions are the same?
At equilibrium the rate of the forward reaction is _______ the rate of reverse reaction.
The equilibrium N2(g) + O2(g) ⇌ 2NO(g), is an example of _______ chemical equilibrium.
H2O(l) ⇌ H2O(g) is in _______ chemical equilibrium.
From the given three statements, which one is NOT true about the following reaction? N2(g) + 3H2(g) ⇌ 2NH3(g).
a: It is a heterogeneous equilibrium reaction
It is a homogeneous equilibrium reaction

q: Homogeneous chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are all in ______.
Heterogenous chemical equilibrium, the reactants and the products of chemical equilibrium are all in _______.
Equilibrium in which all substances are in the same phases are called _______.
Equilibrium in which two or more phases are present in the reaction are called _______.
The reaction used to manufacture ammonia from nitrogen and hydrogen is reversible. Which statement describes the equilibrium?
a: Both the forward reaction and the backward reaction have the same rate
The rate of the backward reaction is greater than the rate of the forward reaction
The rate of the forward reaction is greater than the rate of the backward reaction
The forward and backward reactions have both stopped

q: Which statement about a reaction in equilibrium is not correct?
a: Both the forward and the backward reactions are proceeding at the same rate
There is no further change in the net concentration
The reactions continuous to take place
The amount of product present is only affected by a change in pressure

q: Methane reacts with steam and an equilibrium is reached. CH4(g) + H2O(l) ⇌ CO(g) + 3H2(g) Delta H = (+). Which increases yield of hydrogen?
An equilibrium reaction is shown. N2O4(g) ⇌ 2NO2(g) Delta H = (+). What is the effect of increasing temperature on the equilibrium position?
An equilibrium reaction is shown. H2(g) + I2(g) ⇌ 2HI(g). What is the effect of increasing pressure on the equilibrium position?
N2O4(g) ⇌ 2NO2(g) Delta H = (+). Which statement is not correct?
a: At equilibrium the concentration of reactants and products are constant
At equilibrium, the rate of the forward reaction is equal to the reverse rate
When pressure is increased, the reaction mixture becomes reddish-brown
When temperature increased, the reaction mixture becomes reddish-brown

q: The forward reaction between nitrogen and oxygen is endothermic. Which change increases the yield of NO?
For endothermic CaCO3(s) ⇌ CaO(s) + CO2(g), Le Chatelier predicts _______ results in increase of CO2.
In which reaction would increasing pressure at constant temperature NOT change concentrations?
Consider CO(g) + 2H2(g) ⇌ CH3OH(g) Delta H = (-). An increase in pressure will result in _______.
a: more yield of CO(g) and H2(g)

q: The effect of a catalyst on an equilibrium is to _______.
a: shift the equilibrium to the right
increase the rate of the forward reaction only
increase the rate at which equilibrium is achieved without changing composition

q: For CaO(s) + CO2(g) ⇌ CaCO3(s) Delta H = (-), highest quantity of product is obtained by carrying out reaction at _______.
Consider 2CO2(g) ⇌ 2CO(g) + O2(g) Delta H = (-). Adding O2(g) to the reaction will favor _______.
A reaction is given by aA + bB ⇌ cC + dD. How do you represent an equilibrium constant?
The relationship between the rate constants for forward and reverse reactions and the equilibrium constant is Keq = _______.
In the equilibrium, _______ can be applied for the gaseous reactions.
The value of Keq is constant only at a specified _______.
Which of the following is not included when writing equilibrium constant expression?
If the Keq value is _______, the equilibrium lies to the right and there are more products than reactants.
If the equilibrium is much _______ one then equilibrium lies to the left.
If the equilibrium is much smaller than one then _______.
a: both reactants and products are equally favoured

q: If the molar concentration are used, the symbol of the equilibrium constant is _______.
For equilibrium that involves gases, the equilibrium constant can be expressed as _______.
What is the effect of increasing pressure on the equilibrium position? H2(g) + I2(g) ⇌ 2HI(g)
